perturbation.original_text: "Approved Merchant" means any Merchant that: (a) Is solicited by ISO (or ISO's Other MSP's or IC's) for participation in the Program; (b) Meets the established criteria for participation in the Program, including the Merchant Processing Policy; and (c) Enters into a Merchant Processing Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Approved Merchant" means any **Client** that: (a) Is solicited by ISO (or ISO's Other MSP's or IC's) for participation in the Program; (b) Meets the established criteria for participation in the Program, including the Merchant Processing Policy; and (c) Enters into a Merchant Processing Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Approved Merchant" is changed to "Client" in the definition. This creates uncertainty because 'Merchant' and 'Client' are used throughout the agreement, and now it's unclear whether they are interchangeable, leading to potential disputes over obligations. This contradiction affects Section 4.1 where 'ISO shall solicit Applications from Eligible Merchants' and now it raises a question, whether Eligible Client or Eligible Merchant should be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Applications. ISO shall solicit Applications from Eligible Merchants at ISO's sole expense and shall provide each Applicant with Application Materials. ISO shall collect completed and signed Application Materials and Merchant Processing Agreements from Applicants and shall forward them to SERVICERS within [***] [***] Business Days after the merchant is boarded (which may be accessed by SERVICERS online or forwarded to SERVICERS via ISO's automated application system or to a facsimile number designated by SERVICERS, provided such complies with the Rules and applicable laws, rules and regulations) or to any other place(s) as SERVICERS may designate for processing and document storage. ISO shall retain copies of all Application Materials, Merchant Processing Agreements and documents forwarded to SERVICERS. ISO shall provide SERVICERS with access to ISO's automated application system and ISO's online Merchant Account system (BAMS, or any such successor or replacement system) at no cost to SERVICERS.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Approved Merchant" to "Client" in the definition creates a direct conflict with Section 4.1, which states "ISO shall solicit Applications from Eligible Merchants." The modification introduces ambiguity regarding whether 'Merchants' and 'Clients' are interchangeable. If they are not, the changed definition contradicts the explicit requirement in Section 4.1 to solicit 'Merchants', potentially making the contract unenforceable due to unclear obligations.

perturbation.original_text: "Application" shall mean the Merchant application used by ISO, as provided by and/or approved by SERVICERS and ISO, and other existing Merchant applications assigned to Bank as part of the initial BIN/ICA transfer.
perturbation.changed_text: "Application" shall mean the **Customer Form** used by ISO, as provided by and/or approved by SERVICERS and ISO, and other existing Merchant applications assigned to Bank as part of the initial BIN/ICA transfer.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Application” to “Customer Form” leads to inconsistency. The agreement consistently uses "Application Materials" and "Application processing procedures." Now, the reader is unsure if “Customer Form Materials” is also a correct term, leading to uncertainties in the document's interpretation. For example, this in-text contradiction is shown in section 3.1 where "Application Materials" are used. Now, a reader can be unsure, if this section is reffering to "Customer Form Materials".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Application Materials. ISO shall use reasonable efforts to ensure that each Application completed by an Applicant is current and contains accurate and complete information. ISO's Application Materials shall comply with all applicable Rules, laws and regulations. All Application Materials and any changes to the form and content thereof must be approved in writing by SERVICERS prior to use by ISO, which approval will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed beyond [***] [***] days unless such changes *** Omitted pursuant to a confidential treatment request.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Application" to "Customer Form" introduces misaligned terminology. The original text defines "Application" in a way that includes other merchant applications. The changed text substitutes "Application" with "Customer Form," leading to potential confusion and inconsistency when the document refers to "Application Materials" in section 3.1. Now, it's unclear whether "Application Materials" refers to "Customer Form Materials." This creates a direct conflict and makes the interpretation of obligations related to application materials ambiguous, thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: "Program Standards" means the policies and procedures established by SERVICERS to be used by ISO in connection with the solicitation of prospective Merchants and other policies, procedures, fines and penalties established by SERVICERS that are designed to promote the financial safety or soundness of the Program.
perturbation.changed_text: "Program Standards" means the policies and procedures established by SERVICERS to be used by ISO in connection with the solicitation of prospective Merchants and other policies, procedures, fines and penalties established by SERVICERS that are designed to promote the financial safety or soundness of the **Project**.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Program” with “Project” in the definition of “Program Standards” introduces an in-text contradiction. The contract refers frequently to "Program Participation Fees", as found in SECTION 6.2 Settlement Procedures. It now raises ambiguity on if those are Project Participation Fees or if the original term applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Settlement Procedures. (a) SERVICERS shall settle each Merchant Account in accordance with the terms of the applicable Merchant Processing Agreement, remitting to each Approved Merchant, by ACH or other acceptable method, all settled funds due to the Approved Merchant after first deducting from such settled funds all applicable Program Participation Fees and any other amounts owed or withheld under the applicable Merchant Processing Agreement. Merchant Account settlement is included in the fees set forth in Exhibit A and will not incur an additional fee. (b) On each Payment Date, SERVICERS shall credit ISO's Residual Account in an amount equal to all Net Program Participation Fees collected by SERVICERS since the immediately preceding Payment Date and which have not otherwise been paid to ISO.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of "Program Standards" referred to policies promoting the financial safety of the "Program". The changed text replaces "Program" with "Project," creating a contradiction. Section 6.2, "Settlement Procedures," refers to "Program Participation Fees". By changing the definition of "Program Standards" to now relate to "Project", it creates ambiguity as to whether the fees in Section 6.2 are still applicable, or if they now relate to "Project Participation Fees", thus creating uncertainty and a direct conflict within the document, as described in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
